name: Parpalló Cave Trail
name_original: Senda de la cueva del Parpalló por el mirador de la Caldereta — circular desde La Drova
description: Hike a moderate 5.5-mile loop from La Drova, exploring the Parpalló-Borrell natural park and views from Mirador de la Caldereta.
region_intro: Hiking around Barx offers diverse landscapes characterized by lush pine forests, mountains, and valleys. The terrain includes Mediterranean flora and features historical terraces and old stone constructions. Elevated points provide views of the surrounding region. Best hiking trails around Barx The most popular hiking route is Mirador del Coll de la Rafaela and Cim del Mondúver — circular from La Drova, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views from elevated points. Another top favourite among local hikers is Parpalló Cave Trail, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. The Parpalló Cave Trail terrain includes varied natural features and historical elements. Local hikers also love the Barx Trail through the Manessa Ravine and the Alto de l’Aldaia — circular route through La Drova (PR-CV 60), a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the Manessa Ravine and to the Alto de l’Aldaia, often completed in about 4 hours 16 minutes. Hiking around Barx is defined by pine forests, mountain vistas, and ravines. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Barx are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 230 reviews. More than 1,100 hikers have used komoot to explore Barx's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Barx?
A: Barx offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 29 routes recorded on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Barx?
A: The terrain around Barx is diverse, characterized by lush pine forests, mountains, and valleys. You'll encounter Mediterranean flora, historical terraces, and old stone constructions. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1376104386/parpallo-cave-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parpalló Cave Trail&lt;/a&gt; offer varied natural features, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1376102670/barx-trail-through-the-manessa-ravine-and-the-alto-de-laldaia-circular-route-through-la-drova-pr-cv-60" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barx Trail through the Manessa Ravine&lt;/a&gt; leads through a notable ravine.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Barx?
A: Yes, Barx has several options suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. There are 3 easy routes available, perfect for a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Barx?
A: Many of the trails in Barx are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353840613/mirador-del-coll-de-la-rafaela-and-cim-del-monduver-circular-from-la-drova" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Coll de la Rafaela and Cim del Mondúver — circular from La Drova&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352526378/from-barx-to-la-drova-pr-cv-60-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Barx to La Drova (PR-CV 60) — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Barx?
A: The region around Barx is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, ensuring you carry sufficient water. Winter hiking is also possible, with generally mild conditions.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Barx?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in the Barx area. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to check specific trail regulations if available.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options suitable for children in Barx?
A: While specific stroller-friendly routes are limited due to the natural terrain, many of the easier and moderate trails can be enjoyed by families with older children. The diverse landscapes and opportunities to spot wildlife make for an engaging experience.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I see while hiking near Barx?
A: Hikers can discover various points of interest. Elevated points like Alt de l'Aldaia offer spectacular 360-degree views, potentially including the distant islands of Ibiza and Formentera on clear days. You can also explore historical elements like ancient terraces. Nearby, you might visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387311" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducal Palace of the Borgias in Gandia&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4027411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bairén Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Barx?
A: Public transport options directly to specific trailheads in Barx can be limited. It is generally recommended to have private transportation to access the starting points of many hiking routes in the area, or to check local bus schedules for connections to Barx village itself.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Barx?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 230 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, stunning panoramic views from elevated points, and the peaceful atmosphere of the pine forests.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Barx?
A: Barx village offers amenities such as cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after a hike. There are also accommodation options available in and around the village for those looking to extend their stay and explore the region further.

Q: Where can I find viewpoints with panoramic vistas in Barx?
A: Many trails in Barx lead to elevated points offering stunning views. The climb to the 751-meter-high Alt de l'Aldaia is particularly noted for its spectacular 360-degree views of the Drova valley and beyond. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353840613/mirador-del-coll-de-la-rafaela-and-cim-del-monduver-circular-from-la-drova" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Coll de la Rafaela&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent panoramic views.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Path of the Cueva del Parpalló by the Mirador de la Caldereta?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 8.9 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 43 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your pace and how long you spend exploring the various points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hiking trail?
faqs.answer: The Path of the Cueva del Parpalló by the Mirador de la Caldereta is rated as moderate. It requires a good level of fitness, but the paths are largely accessible. There's an elevation gain and loss of about 290 meters. While mostly straightforward, some sections, especially if you venture towards Cova Oberta or Penya Negra, can be more challenging.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in La Drova. You can find parking options within La Drova, which serves as the primary access point for this hike.

faqs.question: Is the Path of the Cueva del Parpalló by the Mirador de la Caldereta dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is generally accessible, specific regulations regarding dogs within the Parpalló-Borrell Municipal Natural Area may apply. It's always best to check local signage or the park's official guidelines for the most current information on leash requirements and restricted areas to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable for hiking during most seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended, while winter can be cooler but still suitable for hiking on clear days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail or visit the Parpalló-Borrell Municipal Natural Area?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no permit or entrance fee required to hike the trails within the Parpalló-Borrell Municipal Natural Area. However, if you plan to take a guided tour of the Cueva del Parpalló itself, there may be a fee and advance booking required. We recommend checking the Visit Gandia website for the latest information on cave tours.

faqs.question: What interesting sights can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers several notable points of interest. You'll experience the Ascent Towards Tossal de la Caldereta, pass by the Font del Garrof, and enjoy panoramic views from the Coll de la Radella Viewpoint and the Caldereta Viewpoint. You can also see the historic Lime Kiln of la Drova. The highlight of the area is the Cueva del Parpalló, a significant prehistoric site.

faqs.question: Can I visit the Cueva del Parpalló during the hike?
faqs.answer: The trail passes near the Cueva del Parpalló, which is one of Europe's most important Upper Paleolithic prehistoric sites. Guided visits to the cave are often available, typically starting from an information center nearby. It's advisable to check the Visit Gandia website for tour schedules and booking information, as direct access might be restricted.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on this path?
faqs.answer: The path primarily consists of accessible trails, winding through the limestone rock formations of the Mondúver Massif. While generally well-maintained, some sections require sure-footedness. The route offers a diverse natural environment within the Parpalló-Borrell municipal natural park.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates significant portions of other official trails. Approximately 84% of the path follows the Senda de la Cova del Parpalló (SL-CV 79), and it also briefly intersects with sections of the [CIMA VA01] Montdúver * Gandía and De Barx a la Drova trails.

faqs.question: Is this a suitable trail for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and accessible paths, the main circular route can be suitable for families with older children who are accustomed to hiking. However, the full duration and elevation gain should be considered. Younger children or those less experienced might find it challenging. Always assess your family's fitness level before embarking on the full trail.

waypoints.name: Ascent Towards Tossal de la Caldereta
